note: ROOT CAUSE FOUND & RESOLVED — SUPERSEDES the Jun-9 'read-only key' diagnosis and today's 'account entitlement' hypotheses: the 3-session order-write block was a TradeZero BREAKING SCHEMA CHANGE on POST /v1/api/accounts/{acct}/order (changed ~Jun 8 close -> Jun 9 open). A body missing these dies in gateway schema validation BEFORE auth with the misleading 400 invalid-character-'<' (hence the no-auth probe returning the identical error — it never reached auth either). Keys were NEVER the problem; owner supplied the correct format and orders worked instantly. WORKING SCHEMA (validated live): {clientOrderId, side:Buy|Sell, openClose:Open|Close, symbol, orderQuantity, orderType:Limit|Market, route:'SMART', timeInForce:'Day', securityType:'Stock', limitPrice}. Notes: Sell/Open auto-converts to SellShort; paper echoes route:'PAPER'; cancel = DELETE /orders/{clientOrderId}; under SSR a short limit must rest at/above the ask (slow fill). DIAGNOSIS LESSON: a gateway parse error can be SCHEMA validation, not auth — when a previously-working endpoint 400s opaquely, re-check the vendor's CURRENT docs/schema FIRST before concluding permissions; the identical-error-without-auth probe does NOT prove an auth-layer failure when body validation precedes auth.

note: CRITICAL EXIT-EXECUTION BUG (top lesson): a cover (buy-to-close) order priced BELOW a rising market NEVER FILLS -- it is a take-profit, not a stop. To cap a loss on PLSM I placed a cover BUY-LIMIT @5.45 at 10:28 when last was 5.37-5.45; the dead-tape grind-up carried price UP past 5.45 to a 6.38 high, so the limit sat stranded UNFILLED ~3 hours and I stayed short the whole time. Intended ~-$6.6k stop became a -$11,850 realized loss (covered 5.80). HARD FIX: any RISK-EXIT cover MUST be MARKETABLE -- buy at/through the ask (limit a few % above ask, NOT ~20%+ which price-band rejects, or a Market order) -- never a resting buy-limit below market when you want OUT. ALWAYS re-verify position==flat after a cover; never assume a resting limit covered you. Symmetric to the entry rule (don't rest a short above a falling market): exits must be marketable in the direction of urgency.

note: BY-PRINCIPLE LOWER LIMIT must be read from the TRADED share's OWN chart, never another's. Owner mistake today on PLSM: while setting PLSM's by-principle low, was looking at SCAG's chart and set it at 4.50 based on SCAG's curve/values. PLSM's OWN chart showed it had ALREADY reached its reasonable by-principle low at 4.75 on its first drop -- profit should have been taken there. The by-principle low is the lowest place THIS share reasonably reaches (usually above its pre-spike base), where it stalls WITHOUT reversing; take reasonable profit there (Ecclesiastes 5:10). A stall is not a reversal -- judge by experience + close attention; and taking by-principle profit does not end the day (may re-short the same name's continued fall while impatience hours remain).

note: ADD A BORROW-FEE CHECK TO SELECTION (owner-stressed; matters most on a SMALL account). Borrowing to short ~half the owner's small real acct cost ~$1.50 for NEXR (320 sh) vs ~$10.50 for ILLR (just 100 sh). A high per-100-share fee (a) REDUCES profit -- the stock must fall further to net the same -- and (b) RAISES risk -> START with a LOWER upper-limit risk (tighter initial stop) and TRAIL it DOWN as fast as possible after entry. On a small account a $10.50 fee on a ~$500 position is ~2%+ drag (must drop ~2% just to break even on the fee). So a high fee is another mark against a marginal/no-momentum name and should weight go/no-go + stop placement. Prefer low-fee clean-momentum names. ACTION: pull the short/borrow fee in the pre-trade scan and factor it in.
